ilm_policies._comment: Index Lifecycle Management policies for all Zippyra ES indices

rollover._comment: products index does not roll over — it is a single permanent index with routing

warm._comment: products index does not move to warm — always needs fast access

orders_search_ilm._comment: Order search index ages out old orders to save space. Recent 90 days stay hot.

support_tickets_ilm._comment: Tickets older than 2 years move to warm storage, deleted after 5 years

users_search_ilm._comment: User index never deletes (user records are retained). Just force-merges periodically.

warm._comment: Move inactive user shards to warm after 1 year with no index writes

primary_cluster.name: zippyra-es-prod
primary_cluster._comment: AWS recommends OpenSearch (ES fork) for managed deployments. API-compatible with ES 7.10.

master._comment: Dedicated master nodes — never use data nodes as master

data_hot._comment: products index is hot always — NVMe-backed gp3

pii_cluster.name: zippyra-es-pii
pii_cluster._CRITICAL: Separate VPC subnet from primary cluster. No VPC peering to primary cluster. Only Admin Service service account has access.

products_indexing.trigger: Kafka consumer: catalog.product_updated → Catalog Service ES worker (es-indexing-cg)
steps: 2. Fetch full product from PostgreSQL (for fields not in Kafka payload)
3. Fetch current effective_price from pricing_rules table
4. Fetch in_stock + stock_qty from Redis product_availability:{store_id}:{sku_id}
6. Compute has_offer + offer_badge_text from Redis offer_rules:{store_id}
7. Index document with routing=store_id via products_write alias
8. Update store_catalog_version:{store_id} in Redis with new hash

bulk_import_indexing.approach: Parallel bulk indexing with 10 worker goroutines per store
steps: 1. On catalog.import_job event: start worker pool
2. Read products from PostgreSQL in batches of 500
3. Enrich each batch: pricing + stock + offers in parallel
4. Bulk index via products_write alias with routing=store_id

stock_sync.purpose: Sync in_stock and stock_qty from Redis to ES to keep search results accurate
stock_sync.approach: Partial update (update_by_query) on changed SKUs only — never full reindex
stock_sync.query: Update products WHERE store_id=X AND sku_id IN (changed_skus_from_redis_scan)

critical_alerts.action: Page SRE immediately. Search completely down.
Some shard replicas missing. Investigate unassigned shards.
Search SLA breach. Check hot threads, GC pressure, shard sizes.
Catalog updates not reaching search fast enough. Check es-indexing-cg lag.
GC pressure imminent. Scale data nodes or reduce shard count.
Trigger ILM warm tier migration. Scale storage.
Data node down or storage full. Investigate immediately.
Possible cluster unavailability. Check API Gateway → ES connectivity.
